definition: Index trajectories with metric-space structures, approximations, grid/geohash fingerprints, or filter-and-refine bounds. Candidate trajectories are retrieved cheaply, then the intended trajectory distance is computed exactly for final SPECTRA edges.

scale_profile.best_for: Large trajectory or route datasets where Frechet, Hausdorff, edit, or spatiotemporal distances are expensive.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Hundreds of thousands to millions of trajectories, depending on trajectory length and index quality.
scale_profile.avoid_when: Temporal alignment and coordinate systems are inconsistent or the distance is not metric and no valid bounds exist.

tunable_parameters.name: grid_or_geohash_resolution
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls fingerprint granularity for cheap candidate search.

quality_gates: Verify that the approximation or index does not drop exact neighbors.
Report coordinate projection, time normalization, and distance function.
Compare index results with brute force on a subset.
failure_modes: Map projection or time normalization can dominate the measured novelty.
Loose trajectory approximations can produce too many candidates.
Non-metric distances invalidate metric-tree pruning guarantees.
example_usage: Discretize trajectories into grid or geohash tokens, run minhash_lsh_jaccard.py for candidates, then rerank with the selected trajectory distance.

citations.title: Exact Trajectory Similarity Search With N-tree: An Efficient Metric Index for kNN and Range Queries
citations.evidence: Introduces a trajectory distance, N-tree metric index, kNN/range search, and filter-and-refine approximations for expensive trajectory similarity.

citations.title: A Practical Index Structure Supporting Frechet Proximity Queries Among Trajectories
citations.evidence: Develops an index structure for efficient exact and approximate Frechet distance queries on trajectories.
